title: A Princess and a Rebel Leader
description: On pages 9–12, this part focuses on a princess and a rebel leader, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: A Princess and a Rebel Leader. Vivi’s childhood bond with Koza and the roots of the rebel movement give the civil war a personal face. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: You filthy low- lives!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Father... Igaram...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Are you hurt anywhere, Princess Vivi?

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: How dare you try to lay a hand on my precious daughter!

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: You WRE- TCHED SCUM BAGS!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: It appears this fellow was another member of their gang.

dialogue.speaker: Chaka
dialogue.text: Chaka,

dialogue.speaker: Pell
dialogue.text: Pell.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: What're you two doing here?

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: We should be asking your majesty the same question.

dialogue.speaker: Vivi
dialogue.text: What brings the king of a country so far away from his palace?

dialogue.speaker: King Cobra
dialogue.text: We beg your majesty to refrain from personally taking these matters into your hands when we're around.

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Igaram! Kouza is...!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Yes, I'll bring a doctor immediately to treat Kouza,

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: As well as the other members of the Sand- Sand gang.

dialogue.speaker: Pell
dialogue.text: Chaka! Pell! Go call a doctor!

dialogue.speaker: Toto
dialogue.text: Yes, sir!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: What brave little children, they are...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: What's wrong, Vivi? How long are you gonna keep on crying?

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: B-but... It was... So scary...!

dialogue.speaker: Zoro
dialogue.text: Well yeah, it was scary for me too since they had knives but-

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: No, that's not it!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Back then, you said, "We'll die protecting Vivi if we have to..."

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: We'll die protect- ing vivi if we have to!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Please don't ever say,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Some- thing as scary as dying, leader...!

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: So she was scared most by the idea of losing her friends...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: For that, I must give you a proper thank-you.

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: ...Vivi's our friend so it's only natural I did what I did.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: It appears the next generation of Alabama led by children like you will be quite dependable...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I see...

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: ...Do you love this country, Kouza?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Yup! It's the country I was born in, after all!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Same here!

summary: This part develops a princess and a rebel leader and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
